For example, W-AL-02 fired despite the presence of Monte Carlo uncertainty quantification and documented sensitivity analysis; W-CON-01 fired despite all factors being assessed or explicitly marked as not-applicable; W-EP-01 fired despite all claims having clear provenance chains; and W-ON-02 fired despite the COU having a well-defined envelope. The misfirings suggest these rules are overly sensitive to standard credibility factor patterns, triggering even in clean packages. This is a clear case of EXISTING-RULE-MISBEHAVIOR requiring Phase 2.5-style rule predicate refinement.
alternative_rule_analysis: The rules that fired (W-AL-02, W-CON-01, W-EP-01, W-ON-02) were considered and rejected as false positives. Their semantics do not match any defeater in the package. For instance, W-CON-01 should not fire when a factor is explicitly marked as not-applicable with a rationale, and W-AL-02 should not fire when uncertainty quantification and sensitivity analysis are documented. The COMPOUND rules are aggregators and inherit the false positives of their constituent rules.
